title: Draw multiple strokes sequentially without losing existing strokes
description: Verifies a user can draw repeated strokes on the main canvas and each new stroke renders immediately while previous strokes remain visible.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Select a pen tool from the toolbar
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Drag on the canvas to draw an additional stroke
Verify multiple strokes are rendered on the canvas and remain visible

title: Draw a single stroke and see it render immediately
description: Verifies a user can draw a stroke with a pen tool and the stroke appears on the canvas immediately after drawing.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Select a pen tool from the toolbar
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Verify the stroke is rendered on the canvas

title: Open pen picker via double-tap and apply color, width, and pen type to subsequent strokes
description: Verifies double-tapping a pen tool opens the pen picker and chosen color, width, and pen type apply to new strokes while the toolbar reflects the updated settings.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Double-tap a pen tool in the toolbar
Select a color from the palette
Adjust the stroke width slider
Choose a different pen type
Close the pen picker dialog
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Verify the toolbar reflects the selected pen settings and the new stroke uses the updated settings

title: Switch pen tools and ensure only subsequent strokes use the current tool
description: Verifies switching between pen tools changes only future strokes and does not retroactively alter previously drawn strokes.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Select one pen tool from the toolbar
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Select a different pen tool from the toolbar
Drag on the canvas to draw another stroke
Verify both strokes remain visible and the later stroke uses the currently selected tool settings

title: Change only stroke width and keep the previously selected color
description: Verifies adjusting only stroke width in the pen picker updates width for new strokes while keeping the existing color unchanged.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Double-tap a pen tool in the toolbar
Select a color from the palette
Close the pen picker dialog
Double-tap the same pen tool in the toolbar
Adjust the stroke width slider
Close the pen picker dialog
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Verify the selected pen color remains the same while the stroke width reflects the new setting

title: Drawing works when lasso selection overlay is present
description: Verifies that when the lasso selection overlay is visible/active, drawing input is not unexpectedly blocked and the user can still produce a stroke when intending to draw.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Activate the lasso selection overlay from the toolbar
Select a pen tool from the toolbar
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Verify a stroke is rendered on the canvas

title: Close pen picker by tapping outside and keep last applied settings
description: Verifies the pen picker can be dismissed by an outside tap and the currently applied pen settings remain in effect after closing.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Double-tap a pen tool in the toolbar
Select a color from the palette
Tap outside the pen picker dialog to dismiss it
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Verify the new stroke uses the last selected pen settings

title: Drawing at canvas edges still produces visible strokes
description: Verifies a user can draw near the canvas boundaries and the stroke remains visible without being lost or clipped unexpectedly.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Select a pen tool from the toolbar
Drag on the canvas near an edge to draw a stroke
Verify the stroke is rendered on the canvas

title: Stroke width supports smallest and largest selectable values
description: Verifies the stroke width slider allows selecting minimum and maximum values and new strokes reflect the selected extremes.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Double-tap a pen tool in the toolbar
Set the stroke width slider to the minimum value
Close the pen picker dialog
Drag on the canvas to draw a stroke
Double-tap a pen tool in the toolbar
Set the stroke width slider to the maximum value
Close the pen picker dialog
Verify the stroke drawn at maximum width is visibly thicker than the stroke drawn at minimum width
